skill.name: when-deploying-cloud-swarm-use-flow-nexus-swarm
skill.description: Deploy Flow Nexus cloud agent swarms with workflow orchestration, monitoring scripts, and autoscaling guidance.
skill.summary: Guides setup, deployment, monitoring, and scaling for Flow Nexus cloud agent swarms.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-severity patterns, but the weak-crypto, C2, and reconnaissance alerts are mostly false positives from diagram labels, words such as design, and workflow terminology. Confirmed risk remains medium because the skill instructs users to run external commands, create executable shell scripts, call Flow Nexus cloud APIs, and enable autoscaling that can affect resources and cost.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs users to run npx, shell commands, and Flow Nexus MCP commands. This is legitimate for a deployment SOP, but users must review commands before execution because they can install packages, create resources, and trigger cloud workflows.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and numerous, including npx invocations and MCP cloud operations. They appear aligned with the skill purpose, so this is an operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Executable Monitoring and Autoscaling Scripts
medium_findings.description: The SOP creates executable shell scripts for monitoring and autoscaling. The autoscaling monitor runs continuously and can change the number of cloud agents, which may increase resource usage or cost if used without limits.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts are directly written with heredocs and made executable with chmod. The autoscaling loop calls swarm_scale based on metrics, creating a clear resource-management risk.

medium_findings.title: External Cloud Service Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill depends on Flow Nexus, E2B sandboxes, and related online documentation. This is expected for cloud deployment, but it means workflow data and resource actions may leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly lists cloud service prerequisites and calls Flow Nexus MCP operations. I did not find evidence of covert exfiltration, but external service use is intrinsic to the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and C2 Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high static alerts for weak cryptography and C2 terminology are caused by normal words and labels such as design, mesh, W-C2, and execution. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, command-and-control behavior, or malicious beaconing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review shows these locations are diagrams, phase descriptions, or workflow labels. There is no code implementing DES, MD5, SHA1, RC4, or C2 communication.

low_findings.title: Operational Identifiers Stored in Memory
low_findings.description: The skill stores swarm, workflow, and execution identifiers in Claude Flow memory. These are not secrets in the reviewed content, but users should avoid storing credentials or sensitive project data with the same pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The memory operations are visible and store IDs or status summaries. This is a low concern because no secret values are shown, but the pattern can be misused by operators.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses npx to run Flow Nexus and Claude Flow commands. Users should pin and verify package versions before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct npx commands are present in setup and phase actions. The behavior is intended, but unpinned package execution is a recognized supply-chain risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Scripts That Call Cloud APIs
dangerous_patterns.description: The generated monitoring scripts call Flow Nexus commands and parse output with jq and bc. Operators should review generated scripts before making them executable.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts are concrete and executable after chmod. Their commands are related to monitoring, but they still run local shell and external platform operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Continuous Autoscaling Loop
dangerous_patterns.description: The autoscaling monitor loops forever and can call swarm_scale when utilization crosses thresholds. Use quotas, manual approval, and monitoring before enabling it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The loop and scaling calls are explicit. The script includes upper and lower bounds, which lowers severity, but it still changes cloud resources automatically.

content.user_title: Deploy Flow Nexus Cloud Agent Swarms
content.value_statement: Complex agent deployments need clear topology, workflow, and monitoring steps. This skill provides a structured Flow Nexus SOP for cloud swarms, scripts, metrics, and scaling.
actual_capabilities: Defines prerequisites for Flow Nexus, Claude Flow, and optional E2B sandboxes.
Guides hierarchical, mesh, ring, and star swarm topology selection.
Shows steps to initialize a cloud swarm and spawn coordinator, supervisor, and worker agents.
Creates workflow, monitoring, performance analysis, and deployment summary artifacts.
Provides autoscaling policy guidance and a shell-based autoscaling monitor.
Stores swarm, workflow, execution, metrics, and completion state in Claude Flow memory.
limitations: Requires a configured Flow Nexus MCP server and authenticated account.
Several commands affect cloud resources and may create cost or quota usage.
Example workflows are generic and must be adapted before production use.
Generated shell scripts require review before execution in sensitive environments.

use_cases.title: Launch a development swarm
use_cases.description: Deploy coordinator, supervisor, and worker agents for a cloud-based development workflow.

use_cases.title: Coordinate event-driven work
use_cases.description: Create Flow Nexus workflows with triggers, task assignment, execution tracking, and audit trails.

use_cases.title: Tune swarm scaling
use_cases.description: Collect metrics, analyze utilization, and apply bounded autoscaling policies for agent capacity.

prompt_templates.title: Check readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to check whether my environment is ready for a Flow Nexus cloud swarm. List missing prerequisites and safe next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a swarm deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a Flow Nexus swarm for my workflow. Recommend topology, agent roles, monitoring needs, and resource limits.

prompt_templates.title: Create deployment artifacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prepare the configuration, workflow outline, monitoring scripts, and deployment summary for a cloud agent swarm.

prompt_templates.title: Review autoscaling safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit my Flow Nexus autoscaling plan. Identify cost risks, runaway scaling risks, and approval gates before execution.

output_examples.input: Prepare a Flow Nexus swarm for a full-stack development workflow.
output_examples.output: A deployment plan with coordinator, supervisor, and worker roles, workflow phases, monitoring checkpoints, and scaling policy notes.

output_examples.input: Review whether my swarm is ready to scale from 8 to 10 agents.
output_examples.output: A readiness assessment covering utilization, response time, error rate, quota impact, and rollback steps.

output_examples.input: Create an operations summary after deployment.
output_examples.output: A concise summary of topology, deployed agents, workflow status, monitoring coverage, performance metrics, and next actions.

best_practices: Review every generated command and script before execution.
Set resource quotas and approval gates before enabling autoscaling.
Keep swarm IDs and workflow IDs separate from credentials and secrets.
anti_patterns: Do not run unreviewed npx commands in production environments.
Do not enable continuous autoscaling without cost and quota limits.
Do not store API keys or customer data in workflow memory values.

faq.question: What does this skill deploy?
faq.answer: It guides deployment of Flow Nexus cloud agent swarms with workflows, monitoring, and scaling steps.

faq.question: Does it require Flow Nexus?
faq.answer: Yes. The SOP assumes a Flow Nexus MCP server and an authenticated Flow Nexus account.

faq.question: Does it create local files?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions create configuration files, monitoring scripts, reports, and a deployment summary.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: It uses external commands, cloud APIs, executable scripts, and autoscaling. No malicious intent was found.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools, but Flow Nexus access is still required.

faq.question: What should I review before running it?
faq.answer: Review package versions, cloud account permissions, script contents, scaling limits, and memory values.
